claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) reduced deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.

result.claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) reduced deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.
result.reasoning: The claim that thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) reduced deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke is refuted by strong evidence from the CLOTS Trials. Specifically, Paper 23482600, an international multicentre randomized controlled trial (CLOTS Trials 1 and 2) with a large sample size of 5532 immobile stroke patients, found no statistically significant differences in venous thromboembolism events between treatment groups (thigh-length GCS vs. no GCS in Trial 1, and thigh-length vs. below-knee GCS in Trial 2). The study concluded that "No other long-term benefits were apparent" regarding the use of thigh-length stockings. While Paper 49660920, a smaller RCT (sample size 88), showed a numerical difference in DVT incidence between thigh-high (26.8%) and knee-high (38.3%) stockings in hemorrhagic stroke patients, it explicitly stated there was "no significant difference (P = 0.25) observed." The robust findings from the CLOTS trials, with their large sample size and direct relevance to the claim, provide strong evidence against it.

evidence.text: The use of prothrombin complex concentrate can reduce the severity of pathological changes in the hemostasis in patients with traumatic injuries.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: 88 patients with hemorrhagic stroke were randomly assigned to two groups to receive either thigh-high or knee-high graduated compression stockings.
evidence.source: Comparison of Knee-High and Thigh-High Graduated Compression Stockings in Preventing Deep Vein Thrombosis in Patients with Hemorrhagic Stroke

evidence.text: In CLOTS Trial 1, 2518 immobile stroke patients were allocated thigh-length graduated compression stockings or not.
evidence.source: The effect of graduated compression stockings on long-term outcomes after stroke: the CLOTS trials 1 and 2.

evidence.text: The study examined venous thromboembolic disease in postoperative neurosurgical and trauma patients in ICU.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: The study aimed to compare the effectiveness of knee-high and thigh-high graduated compression stockings in preventing DVT in patients with hemorrhagic stroke.
evidence.source: Comparison of Knee-High and Thigh-High Graduated Compression Stockings in Preventing Deep Vein Thrombosis in Patients with Hemorrhagic Stroke

evidence.text: In the knee-high stockings group, 18 patients (38.3%) were affected by DVT during the study.
evidence.source: Comparison of Knee-High and Thigh-High Graduated Compression Stockings in Preventing Deep Vein Thrombosis in Patients with Hemorrhagic Stroke

evidence.text: Dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin + clopidogrel 75 mg/d) is a potential treatment for APS patients following a first episode of arterial thrombosis.
evidence.source: Antiphospholipid antibodies and atherosclerotic vascular disease: recent advances.

evidence.text: D. Markopoulou researched the contribution of cytokines on thrombosis development during hospitalization in ICU.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: In the thigh-high stockings group, 11 patients (26.8%) were affected by DVT during the study.
evidence.source: Comparison of Knee-High and Thigh-High Graduated Compression Stockings in Preventing Deep Vein Thrombosis in Patients with Hemorrhagic Stroke

evidence.text: Bleeding and thrombosis outcomes were studied in hospitalized COVID-19 patients on low-molecular-weight heparin and antiplatelet therapy.
evidence.source: Autopsy Findings and Venous Thromboembolism in Patients With COVID-19

evidence.text: In CLOTS trial 2, 3014 patients were allocated to thigh-length or below-knee graduated compression stockings.
evidence.source: The effect of graduated compression stockings on long-term outcomes after stroke: the CLOTS trials 1 and 2.

evidence.text: Meta-analysis research by M. Liu et al. published in 2014 indicated a 25% reduction in the risk of deep vein thrombosis.
evidence.source: Homocysteine, Vitamin B12 and Folate Level: Possible Risk Factors in the Progression of Chronic Heart and Kidney Disorders.

evidence.text: The risk of deep vein thrombosis would be reduced by 25% (8% to 38%) according to meta-analysis research by M. Liu et al. published in 2014.
evidence.source: Homocysteine, Vitamin B12 and Folate Level: Possible Risk Factors in the Progression of Chronic Heart and Kidney Disorders.

evidence.text: The CLOTS Trial 1 included the intervention of thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) or no GCS.
evidence.source: Can clinical features distinguish between immobile patients with stroke at high and low risk of deep vein thrombosis? Statistical modelling based on the CLOTS trials cohorts.

evidence.text: Cancer patients with severe sepsis/septic shock had similar hypercoagulability after a delayed clot initiation as compared to cancer patients without sepsis.
evidence.source: 36th International Symposium on Intensive Care and Emergency Medicine : Brussels, Belgium. 15-18 March 2016.

evidence.text: The study examined venous thromboembolic disease in postoperative neurosurgical and trauma patients in ICU.

evidence.text: The results effectively exclude a >10% relative reduction in the hazard of death within 6 months associated with the use of thigh-length stockings.
evidence.source: The effect of graduated compression stockings on long-term outcomes after stroke: the CLOTS trials 1 and 2.

evidence.text: There was no significant difference (P = 0.25) observed between the thigh-high and knee-high stockings groups in the incidence of DVT.
evidence.source: Comparison of Knee-High and Thigh-High Graduated Compression Stockings in Preventing Deep Vein Thrombosis in Patients with Hemorrhagic Stroke
